name: Chinese Egg Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together 1/2 cup soy sauce, 1/4 cup water, mashed garlic, and juice from 1/4 lemon. Add the chicken and pork. Cover tightly and marinate overnight.
2. Roast the chicken and pork in a preheated oven at 350°F until done (chicken takes about an hour, pork takes about an hour and a half).
3. When cool, cut into matchstick pieces. Set aside.
4. In a wok or deep-sided frying pan, warm 3 tbsp vegetable oil over medium heat.
5. Add the celery, cabbage, onions, and mushrooms. Stir-fry until celery and onions are tender.
6. Add the chicken and pork. Stir until heated through.
7. Add soy sauce, salt, and pepper to taste. Remove from heat.
8. Stir in bean sprouts.
9. To fill each roll, mound about 2 heaping tablespoons of filling just below the center of an egg roll wrapper.
10. Fold the bottom corner up over the filling to cover, then fold in the two outside corners. Roll closed, sealing shut with a bit of egg white.
11. Heat oil in a wok or large pan to a depth of about 2 inches. Over medium-high heat, carefully add the egg rolls one at a time. Deep fry for about 2-3 minutes until golden brown on both sides, turning once.
12. Serve hot with dipping sauce.
DIPPING SAUCE:
1/2 cup Soy sauce
1 tsp Dry mustard
1 tsp Garlic powder
1 tsp Vinegar
1 tsp Brown sugar
